sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, is a 2-month-old nonhealing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has recently become red and is draining.
The patient reports that he got the ulcer from walking barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach.
He denies any specific pain but reports throbbing and increased pressure when he touches the area.
The patient's biggest concern is the ulcer's redness and drainage.
He has a history of diabetes, which he believes is the cause of the ulcer's poor healing.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes and takes medication for it, but he does not check his blood sugar levels regularly.
His last A1c was around 10%.
The patient has not had any recent antibiotic prescriptions filled.
Review of systems: The patient reports no fever, but has a history of diabetes.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, and he does not have a fever.
Physical exam: The patient has a one by two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The wound is red, swollen, and has drainage.
There is edema around the wound, but no necrosis, odor, or bony exposure.
The patient reports increased pressure when the wound is touched.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with signs of infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Infection (suspected); Poorly controlled diabetes (status: unknown); No antibiotic prescription filled (status: unknown).
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400 milligrams twice a day for seven days to treat the infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will order an ABI to determine the blood supply to the foot.
I will provide the patient with a surgical shoe to wear while the ulcer heals.
The patient will return in two weeks for follow-up and possible additional debridement.
Follow-up: The patient will return in two weeks for follow-up.
